name: Chunk Lock
tagline: Lightweight chunk claiming for survival servers and modpacks.
summary: Simple land protection designed around ownership, persistence, and multiplayer safety without economies, factions, towns, or bloated management systems.
bestFor: Survival servers, SMP worlds, lightweight protection setups, and utility-focused modpacks.

name: Stacksmith
tagline: Stack identical tools without breaking vanilla durability behavior.
summary: Reduces inventory clutter by allowing matching tools to stack while preserving normal usage and durability mechanics.
bestFor: Mining, exploration, long survival sessions, and inventory-heavy modpacks.

name: Pocket Filter
summary: Whitelist and blacklist item pickup filtering built for mining, farms, builders, and players tired of inventory spam.
bestFor: Mining sessions, mob farms, builders, exploration, and cleanup-heavy gameplay.

name: AutoLatch
tagline: Doors automatically close after a configurable delay.
summary: A lightweight utility mod that keeps bases, villages, and multiplayer builds cleaner by automatically closing doors after use.
bestFor: Builders, villages, SMP servers, roleplay worlds, and general survival convenience.

name: Encumbrance
tagline: Inventory weight simulation built for survival realism.
summary: Adds configurable inventory burden mechanics to make resource management and exploration feel heavier and more deliberate.
bestFor: Survival realism packs, slower progression experiences, and exploration-focused gameplay.

name: P.U.P - Passive Until Provoked
tagline: Hostile mobs remain neutral until attacked.
summary: Adjusts hostile mob behavior so worlds feel calmer without completely removing danger or replacing vanilla combat mechanics.
bestFor: Builders, casual survival worlds, family servers, and lower-stress gameplay setups.

name: TorchRedux
tagline: Tick acceleration utility for underserved version targets.
summary: A lightweight tick acceleration utility inspired by older automation mods while targeting newer Forge and NeoForge ecosystems.
bestFor: Technical players, automation packs, and newer-version utility-focused setups.
